name: New Jersey Office of Information Technology (NJOIT)
tagline: New Jersey's central IT agency: NJOIT is the exclusive IT provider for the Executive Branch, running enterprise infrastructure, the state data center, cloud, cybersecurity, and shared services for dozens of departments and agencies — and home to the state CTO.

snapshot.label: Role
snapshot.value: Central / enterprise IT for Executive Branch

overview: The New Jersey Office of Information Technology (NJOIT) is the state's central technology agency — under the 2007 Office of Information Technology Reorganization Act it is the exclusive government IT provider for New Jersey's Executive Branch, powering the business operations of dozens of departments and agencies in Trenton and across the state. NJOIT runs enterprise infrastructure (including the state data center), networking, cloud, cybersecurity, application development, and shared services, and sets statewide IT policy and standards; it employs 500+ technologists. For a technology seller, NJOIT is the central IT decision-maker and buying center for statewide infrastructure, while the contracting and budget mechanics run through a separate agency — the NJ Department of the Treasury's Division of Purchase & Property (DPP) and the NJSTART portal. The account sits inside a fresh leadership reset: Governor Mikie Sherrill took office in January 2026 and named Kevin Dehmer permanent Chief Technology Officer in February 2026, with an explicit mandate to modernize digital infrastructure, speed permitting and licensing, and strengthen cyber defenses against AI-driven threats.

budget.intro: NJOIT's leverage is operational rather than a single large appropriation line: it runs enterprise infrastructure as a shared service across the Executive Branch and recovers much of its delivery cost from the agencies it serves. The macro pool is the state budget — Governor Sherrill's first budget, an about-$60.7B FY2027 Appropriations Act, was signed at the fiscal-year start (June 30, 2026) — and a flagship NJOIT capital effort, the new Enterprise Data Center, is slated to come online in FY2027.

stats.label: FY2027 enacted state budget
stats.note: Sherrill's first budget, signed June 30, 2026; the pool agency IT and NJOIT services draw from

stats.label: NJOIT technology staff
stats.note: Application development, network/database engineering, security operations

stats.label: Enterprise Data Center target
stats.note: New 24/7 data center for Executive-Branch IT services, expected to complete in FY2027

notes: NJOIT funds much of its delivery through chargebacks/cost recovery on enterprise and shared services rather than one large general-revenue line; agency-level IT spend lives in each department's budget.
Top-line figures reflect the enacted FY2027 Appropriations Act (signed June 30, 2026); departmental IT detail may differ from the proposed budget book.
Confirm NJOIT's own FY2027 appropriation and the Enterprise Data Center funding directly in the OMB budget detail before forecasting — published top-line figures for the agency are limited.

initiatives.name: Enterprise Data Center & infrastructure modernization
initiatives.summary: NJOIT is standing up a new Enterprise Data Center to provide 24/7 access to the IT services and applications state agencies and residents rely on, targeted for FY2027. Infrastructure refresh, hybrid cloud, and resilience are the core motion — a direct ISG and data-center play.

initiatives.name: NJ AI Assistant & responsible-AI program
initiatives.summary: NJOIT, with the NJ Innovation Authority, runs the NJ AI Assistant — a secure genAI environment hosted on state infrastructure (not third-party training data). Since its July 2024 launch ~20,000 state employees have used it, with 1M+ prompts and 80%+ satisfaction by Feb 2026, and a 2026 upgrade. Signals real demand for AI-ready compute under state control.

initiatives.name: Permitting / licensing efficiency & digital government
initiatives.summary: A named CTO priority: improve the efficiency and transparency of state permitting, adopt technology to clear licensure backlogs, and cut wait times. Modernization, automation, and managed-services plays should map to these stated efficiency goals.

initiatives.name: Cybersecurity & NJCCIC
initiatives.summary: Statewide cyber runs through NJOIT and the NJ Cybersecurity & Communications Integration Cell (NJCCIC), with Dehmer's mandate to strengthen defenses against AI-driven threats. The security buying center sits with NJOIT — position secure infrastructure, identity, and resilience here.

procurement.intro: NJOIT is the IT decision-maker, but the contracting mechanics run through Treasury's Division of Purchase & Property (DPP) and the NJSTART portal, not one-off NJOIT RFPs in most cases. Two things to confirm first: an active, correctly scoped NJSTART vendor registration, and which statewide term contract(s) a Dell offering falls under. Cooperative purchasing lets local governments, schools, and authorities ride state awards.

vehicles.name: NJSTART vendor registration (DPP)
vehicles.detail: The mandatory front door — vendors register and maintain a NJSTART profile to bid, hold contracts, and invoice the state electronically. Confirm Dell's registration and categories are current.

vehicles.name: Statewide term contracts (DPP)
vehicles.detail: DPP awards master/term contracts agencies (and NJOIT) buy off without separate RFPs. IT-relevant examples include computer equipment & peripherals, cloud & software solutions, and IT consulting. Verify exact contract numbers/scope before forecasting.

vehicles.name: Cooperative purchasing & national co-ops
vehicles.detail: NJ's Cooperative Purchasing Program (N.J.A.C. 5:34) lets registered counties, municipalities, schools, and authorities ride state term contracts; national co-ops can supplement. Extends a single award across the broader NJ public sector.

powerStructure.name: Kevin Dehmer
powerStructure.title: Chief Technology Officer, NJOIT
powerStructure.note: Named permanent CTO Feb 2026 by Gov. Sherrill (prior NJ Commissioner of Education). Owns central IT, the data center, modernization, permitting/licensing tech, and cyber strategy — the primary technology-strategy relationship for the account.

powerStructure.name: Michael Geraghty
powerStructure.title: State CISO & Director, NJCCIC
powerStructure.note: Confirmed continuing under Sherrill as State CISO and Director of the NJ Cybersecurity & Communications Integration Cell (NJCCIC) — statewide cyber policy, incident response, and threat intel. Note NJCCIC sits organizationally in the Office of Homeland Security & Preparedness (NJOHSP), coordinating with NJOIT; the security buying center spans both.

powerStructure.name: NJOIT Enterprise Services
powerStructure.title: Data center / cloud / network / shared services
powerStructure.note: The delivery organization for consolidated infrastructure and as-a-service across agencies (Deputy CTO Hagen Hottmann leads Enterprise Services per the NJOIT leadership roster) — the buying center for ISG and managed-services plays. Vernon Spencer is COO; Poonam Soans is Chief Data Officer.

powerStructure.name: NJ Treasury — Division of Purchase & Property (DPP)
powerStructure.title: Central procurement / NJSTART owner
powerStructure.note: Runs NJSTART and statewide term contracts — the contracting path for NJOIT buys. Confirm the current Director directly, as the role is not consistently published.

powerStructure.name: Dave Cole
powerStructure.title: State Chief Innovation Officer, NJ Innovation Authority
powerStructure.note: Confirmed by Gov.-elect Sherrill (Jan 2026) to continue as Chief Innovation Officer leading the NJ Innovation Authority (formerly Office of Innovation), which co-runs the NJ AI Assistant and responsible-AI training with NJOIT — an adjacency for AI-infrastructure conversations distinct from the core IT-ops relationship.

news.title: Sherrill signs first FY2027 budget (~$60.7B) at fiscal-year start
news.impact: Enacted FY2027 Appropriations Act firms up the pool NJOIT services draw from; confirm the agency's own appropriation and any Enterprise Data Center / modernization detail in OMB budget detail before forecasting.

news.title: NJCCIC releases 2026 Cyber Threat Assessment
news.impact: New Jersey's statewide cyber cell (NJCCIC, under NJOHSP) publishes its annual threat assessment — reinforces AI-enabled threats and whole-of-state defense as the frame for secure-infrastructure and resilience conversations with NJOIT/NJCCIC. Verify specific findings directly.

news.title: New Jersey upgrades its AI Assistant for state workers
news.impact: NJ AI Assistant reported 1M+ prompts, 300K+ sessions, and 80%+ satisfaction across a 70,000-member workforce — signals durable demand for AI-ready compute hosted on state infrastructure.

news.title: Gov. Sherrill names Kevin Dehmer permanent state CTO (NJOIT)
news.impact: Stable owner of central IT, the data center, modernization, and cyber strategy — rebuild the NJOIT relationship map; this is the IT decision-maker, distinct from Treasury contracting.

news.title: Sherrill administration takes office; statewide leadership reset
news.impact: New administration and IT priorities (affordability, efficiency, modernization) — re-confirm contacts and priorities across NJOIT and the technology buying centers.

dellActions: Run the technology motion through NJOIT and CTO Kevin Dehmer — this is the central IT decision-maker for statewide infrastructure, cloud, and cyber; Treasury/DPP is the contracting path, not the IT buyer.
Lead with ISG and AI-infrastructure against the new Enterprise Data Center (FY2027) and the NJ AI Assistant — position AI-ready compute and managed services hosted on state-controlled infrastructure.

Confirm Dell's NJSTART vendor registration is active and correctly categorized, and verify which DPP statewide term contracts (computer equipment, cloud/software, IT consulting) cover Dell's offerings.
Map the security buying center at NJOIT / NJCCIC and align secure-infrastructure, identity, and resilience offerings to Dehmer's mandate to counter AI-driven cyber threats.

Tie modernization and automation plays to the administration's stated permitting/licensing efficiency goals, and leverage NJ cooperative purchasing to extend a single state award across counties, municipalities, schools, and authorities.
